**Plymouth County** is the westernmost [county](/wiki/County_(United_States) "County (United States)") in the [U.S. state](/wiki/U.S._state "U.S. state") of [Iowa](/wiki/Iowa "Iowa"). As of the [2020 census](/wiki/2020_United_States_census "2020 United States census"), the population was 25,698,[[2]](#cite_note-2020_Census_(County)-2) and was estimated to be 25,825 in 2024.[[3]](#cite_note-QF-3) The [county seat](/wiki/County_seat "County seat") and the largest city is [Le Mars](/wiki/Le_Mars,_Iowa "Le Mars, Iowa").[[4]](#cite_note-NACo-4) Plymouth County was named after [Plymouth, Massachusetts](/wiki/Plymouth,_Massachusetts "Plymouth, Massachusetts").[[5]](#cite_note-5)

Plymouth County is part of the [Sioux City](/wiki/Sioux_City,_Iowa "Sioux City, Iowa"), IA-[NE](/wiki/Nebraska "Nebraska")-[SD](/wiki/South_Dakota "South Dakota") [Metropolitan Statistical Area](/wiki/Sioux_City_metropolitan_area "Sioux City metropolitan area").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Plymouth_County,_Iowa&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

Plymouth County was formed on January 15, 1851.[[6]](#cite_note-6) Settlement began in the county in 1856. In October 1859, the first courthouse was built in Melbourne, formerly located in the southeast quarter of section 34, Plymouth Township, about five to six miles due south of Merrill.[[7]](#cite_note-7) The first public school opened its doors there with 32 pupils. In 1872 the county seat was moved to Le Mars and a courthouse and jail were built there in 1873. The present [Plymouth County Courthouse](/wiki/Plymouth_County_Courthouse_(Iowa) "Plymouth County Courthouse (Iowa)") was built in 1900 of red sandstone.[[8]](#cite_note-History-8)

During the [Great Depression](/wiki/Great_Depression_in_the_United_States "Great Depression in the United States"), farmers in the county organized the Farmers Holiday Group, to keep farm products off the market until the desired price was met. A radical group among them abducted Judge Bradley from his court chamber and threatened to hang him in the front of the courthouse. Bradley was freed, but the governor ordered the national guard to Plymouth County and declared a state of emergency, which effectively ended the group.[[8]](#cite_note-History-8)[[9]](#cite_note-9)

## Geography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Plymouth_County,_Iowa&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Geography")]

### 2020 census

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Plymouth_County,_Iowa&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: 2020 census")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/45/PlymouthCountyIowaPop2020.png/250px-PlymouthCountyIowaPop2020.png)](/wiki/File:PlymouthCountyIowaPop2020.png)

Population of Plymouth County from the U.S. census data

As of the [2020 census](/wiki/2020_United_States_census "2020 United States census"), there were 25,698 people, 10,171 households, and 7,048 families residing in the county.[[23]](#cite_note-23) The [population density](/wiki/Population_density "Population density") was 29.78 inhabitants per square mile (11.5/km2). There were 10,818 housing units at an average density of 12.54 per square mile (4.8/km2). The racial makeup of the county was 90.63% [White](/wiki/White_(U.S._Census) "White (U.S. Census)"), 1.39% [African American](/wiki/African_American_(U.S._Census) "African American (U.S. Census)"), 0.34% [Native American](/wiki/Native_American_(U.S._Census) "Native American (U.S. Census)"), 0.45% [Asian](/wiki/Asian_(U.S._Census) "Asian (U.S. Census)"), 0.46% [Pacific Islander](/wiki/Race_(U.S._Census) "Race (U.S. Census)"), 2.55% from some other races and 4.19% from two or more races. [Hispanic or Latino](/wiki/Hispanic_and_Latino_Americans "Hispanic and Latino Americans") people of any race were 5.82% of the population.[[24]](#cite_note-24)

The median age was 40.6 years; 25.2% of residents were under the age of 18 and 19.1% of residents were 65 years of age or older. For every 100 females there were 100.5 males, and for every 100 females aged 18 and over there were 98.4 males age 18 and over.[[25]](#cite_note-Census2020DP-25)[[26]](#cite_note-Census2020PL-26)

39.5% of residents lived in urban areas, while 60.5% lived in rural areas.[[27]](#cite_note-Census2020DHC-27)

There were 10,171 households in the county, of which 30.6% had children under the age of 18 living in them. Of all households, 56.4% were married-couple households, 17.4% were households with a male householder and no spouse or partner present, and 20.3% were households with a female householder and no spouse or partner present. About 26.3% of all households were made up of individuals and 12.7% had someone living alone who was 65 years of age or older.[[25]](#cite_note-Census2020DP-25)

There were 10,818 housing units, of which 6.0% were vacant. Among occupied housing units, 77.0% were owner-occupied and 23.0% were renter-occupied. The homeowner vacancy rate was 0.8% and the rental vacancy rate was 6.8%.[[25]](#cite_note-Census2020DP-25)

[Hispanic or Latino](/wiki/Hispanic_and_Latino_Americans "Hispanic and Latino Americans") people of any race were 1.32% of the population.

There were 9,372 households 35.70% had children under the age of 18 living with them, 63.30% were [married couples](/wiki/Marriage "Marriage") living together, 6.20% had a female householder with no husband present, and 27.40% were non-families. 24.00% of households were one person and 12.00% were one person aged 65 or older. The average household size was 2.61 and the average family size was 3.12.

The age distribution was 28.30% under the age of 18, 7.20% from 18 to 24, 26.40% from 25 to 44, 22.00% from 45 to 64, and 16.00% 65 or older. The median age was 38 years. For every 100 females there were 98.80 males. For every 100 females age 18 and over, there were 95.70 males.

The median household income was $41,638 and the median family income was $50,009. Males had a median income of $33,566 versus $22,558 for females. The [per capita income](/wiki/Per_capita_income "Per capita income") for the county was $19,442. About 4.40% of families and 6.00% of the population were below the [poverty line](/wiki/Poverty_threshold "Poverty threshold"), including 6.70% of those under age 18 and 6.40% of those age 65 or over.
